message.content:
PR #550 (RECO-803) has an e2e-tests CI failure. The branch is `RECO-803-fix-eslint-fbt-service`.

The change converted positional params to named object params and fixed unnecessary conditions in `src/modules/rest/frequently-bought-together/frequently-bought-together.service.ts`.

1. `git checkout RECO-803-fix-eslint-fbt-service`
2. Run e2e tests: `npx dotenv -e .env.test -- npx jest --runInBand --forceExit --config ./test/jest-e2e.config.ts -- frequently-bought-together 2>&1 | tail -80`
3. Read failures, fix test files or source code
4. Also check types: `npx tsc --noEmit 2>&1 | head -20`
5. Commit --no-verify, push --no-verify
6. Report what you fixed

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

content.text: Now I can see the issue. The PR removed null-coalescing guards from `responseFormatter` functions in the controller, and now `res.frequently_bought_together.length` crashes when `frequently_bought_together` is null/undefined. Let me look at the controller and the service.

The issue is clear. The `responseFormatter` functions on lines 40, 69, and 100 call `.length` on `res.frequently_bought_together` without null guards. The PR likely removed the `?? []` or `?.` guards. Let me check what it looked like before and also look at the test expectations.

Now let me look at the failing tests to understand all 7 failures.

content.text: All 7 failures are about the `responseFormatter` crashing when `frequently_bought_together` is null/undefined. The fix is to add optional chaining in the controller's `responseFormatter` functions. Let me also check what the original code looked like on main.

input.description: Check diff from main for the controller

content.text: The PR removed the null-safe guards (`?.` and `?? 0`/`?? false`) from `responseFormatter`. These guards are needed because `responseFormatter` in `@LogExecution` can receive null/undefined values in edge cases. I need to restore the null-safe access while keeping the rest of the PR's changes intact.
